paper_metadata.title: Exploring the Limits of Transfer Learning with a Unified Text-to-Text Transformer
paper_metadata.abstract: Transfer learning, where a model is first pre-trained on a data-rich task before being fine-tuned on a downstream task, has emerged as a powerful technique in natural language processing (NLP). The effectiveness of transfer learning has given rise to a diversity of approaches, methodology, and practice. In this paper, we explore the landscape of transfer learning techniques for NLP by introducing a unified framework that converts all text-based language problems into a text-to-text format. Our systematic study compares pre-training objectives, architectures, unlabeled data sets, transfer approaches, and other factors on dozens of language understanding tasks. By combining the insights from our exploration with scale and our new ``Colossal Clean Crawled Corpus'', we achieve state-of-the-art results on many benchmarks covering summarization, question answering, text classification, and more. To facilitate future work on transfer learning for NLP, we release our data set, pre-trained models, and code.

extraction_data.innovation: Unified text-to-text framework where all NLP tasks are framed as sequence-to-sequence problems, enabling transfer learning across diverse tasks. Introduces span corruption as a pretraining objective, replacing traditional masked language modeling with contiguous spans of masked tokens.
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Supervised fine-tuning on downstream tasks (e.g., translation, summarization, classification)
extraction_data.extension: Span corruption pre-training objective, where random spans of text are replaced with sentinel tokens and the model is trained to reconstruct the original text.
extraction_data.research_problem: Transfer learning and generalization in NLP across diverse tasks
extraction_data.application: Machine translation, question answering, abstractive summarization, text classification, natural language inference
extraction_data.paper_title: Exploring the Limits of Transfer Learning with a Unified Text-to-Text Transformer

extraction_data.innovation: Unified text-to-text framework that reformulates all NLP tasks into a single text-to-text format, enabling transfer learning across diverse tasks. Uses span corruption as a denoising pretraining objective, differing from prior work by masking contiguous spans rather than individual tokens.
extraction_data.application: Text classification, summarization, translation, question answering, and other NLP tasks
extraction_data.research_problem: Transfer learning and multitask learning in NLP
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Supervised fine-tuning on downstream tasks (e.g., translation, summarization, classification)
extraction_data.extension: Span corruption replaces masked tokens with a single sentinel token, enabling the model to predict entire spans of missing text rather than individual tokens.
extraction_data.paper_title: Exploring the Limits of Transfer Learning with a Unified Text-to-Text Transformer

extraction_data.innovation: Scaling study variant to explore the impact of increased model size on performance while keeping computational budget constant.
extraction_data.application: Text classification, summarization, question answering, translation, and other NLP tasks
extraction_data.extension: Doubled model size (16 layers in encoder/decoder) to study scaling trade-offs between model size and training time.
extraction_data.paper_title: Exploring the Limits of Transfer Learning with a Unified Text-to-Text Transformer

extraction_data.innovation: Scaling study variant to explore the impact of increased model size on performance while keeping computational budget constant.
extraction_data.application: Text classification, summarization, question answering, translation, and other NLP tasks
extraction_data.extension: Quadrupled model size (32 layers in encoder/decoder) to study the limits of scaling model parameters within a fixed computational budget.
extraction_data.paper_title: Exploring the Limits of Transfer Learning with a Unified Text-to-Text Transformer
